title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. penamayorensis
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. penamayorensis, also known as the penamayorensis daffodil, is a subspecies of the common daffodil (Narcissus pseudonarcissus) found in the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins. The health benefits of penamayorensis daffodil include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues. Penamayorensis daffodil can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
